description: Vis sanity harness — fast end-to-end checks that the agent loop, native tool calling, the async-by-default sandbox runtime (await/gather on virtual threads), and the print-only context all work against a given model. NOT a capability benchmark (see swe-bench/4clojure for that); this is a smoke test you run after touching the loop, prompt, or sandbox.

tasks.prompt: List the top-level files in this repo and name the build tool in one sentence.
tasks.rationale: Loop turns over at all: locate (reads) then answer.

tasks.prompt: In ONE run_python call, concurrently read deps.edn and bin/vis using gather + await. Then tell me (a) the svar dependency coordinate from deps.edn and (b) what language bin/vis is written in.
tasks.rationale: Async-by-default: model must use await gather(...) and the deferred-tool runtime must overlap the two reads on virtual threads.

tasks.prompt: Count how many .clj files are under src/ using the tools available in the sandbox, then report the number and how you counted.
tasks.rationale: Python compute over a TOOL result (ls/rg), not os.walk/glob — the sandbox denies real filesystem IO. The classic weak-model trap: reach for os.walk, get nothing, declare the sandbox broken.

tasks.prompt: Create a file named vis_sanity_probe.txt in the repository root containing exactly the line 'sanity-ok', then read it back and confirm its contents. Use a path relative to the workspace root (the sandbox confines writes to it).
tasks.rationale: Mutation path (write) + verify-by-reading-back across replies. Path MUST be workspace-relative — the write tool rejects paths that escape the workspace roots, so /tmp/... fails by design.

tasks.prompt: Find which namespace defines the run-python-block function and tell me the file path and roughly what it does in two sentences.
tasks.rationale: Multi-step navigation: search the whole repo, drill in, summarize.

forbid_global: infrastructure issue that i cannot work around
